articles.title: 乌拉圭联队乌加特膝盖韧带受伤
articles.summary: 曼联证实，乌拉圭中场曼努埃尔·乌加特在世界杯小组赛最后一场对阵西班牙的比赛中膝盖韧带受伤。
articles.originalTitle: Uruguay, Utd's Ugarte suffers knee ligament injury...
articles.originalSummary: Uruguay midfielder Manuel Ugarte has suffered a knee ligament injury in his final World Cup group stage match against Spain, Manchester United have confirmed.

articles.title: 马什的“加拿大英雄”取得了决定性的胜利
articles.summary: 加拿大队击败南非队，赢得历史上首场世界杯淘汰赛胜利并晋级16强后，主教练杰西·马什毫不犹豫地用“英雄”一词来形容他的球队。
articles.originalSummary: After Canada beat South Africa to win its first-ever FIFA World Cup knockout match and advance to the round of 16, head coach Jesse Marsch didn't hesitate to use the word heroes in describing his team.

articles.title: 韩国教练因世界杯退出而辞职，受到批评
articles.summary: 韩国队主教练洪明甫周日辞职，一天前该队在世界杯上迅速被淘汰，并受到该国总统的严厉批评。
articles.originalTitle: South Korea coach quits after WC exit, criticism
articles.originalSummary: South Korea coach Hong Myung-Bo resigned Sunday, a day after the team's quick elimination from the World Cup and following heavy criticism from the country's president.

articles.title: 巴洛贡：美国队正在寻找淘汰赛的“另一种装备”......
articles.summary: 美国前锋福拉林·巴洛贡表示，淘汰赛阶段已经开始，他可以“感受到世界杯周围气氛的不同”。
articles.originalTitle: Balogun: U.S. finding 'another gear' for knockouts...
articles.originalSummary: United States forward Folarin Balogun said he can "feel the difference" in the atmosphere around the World Cup now that the knockout stage has begun.

articles.title: 西班牙威廉姆斯受伤：我最糟糕的日子之一
articles.summary: 西班牙边锋尼科·威廉姆斯表示，在周五对阵乌拉圭的比赛中受伤后，他经历了“我一生中最糟糕的日子之一”，这危及了他未来参加世界杯的机会。
articles.originalTitle: Spain's Williams on injury: One of my worst days
articles.originalSummary: Spain winger Nico Williams said he had suffered "one of the worst days of my life" after an injury against Uruguay on Friday jeopardized his future participation in the World Cup.

articles.title: 体育博彩公司热衷于美国赛进入淘汰赛
articles.summary: 随着世界杯进入淘汰赛阶段，明星阵容强大的法国队和阿根廷队在夺冠赔率上名列前茅，而美国队的积极势头将使体育博彩公司每场比赛都汗流浃背。
articles.originalTitle: Sportsbooks sweat U.S. run entering knockouts
articles.originalSummary: Star-powered France and Argentina top the odds board to win the World Cup as the tournament moves into the knockout stage, while the United States' positive momentum will have sportsbooks sweating more with each game the
